question.text: When the aggregate net purchases of equity shares by FIIs/NRIs/PIOs reaches what threshold below the overall limit, the Reserve Bank issues a Caution List?

answer.text: 5 per cent below the overall limit
3 per cent below the overall limit
1 per cent below the overall limit
2 per cent below the overall limit

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When the aggregate net purchases of equity shares of the Indian company by FIIs/NRIs/PIOs reaches the cut-off point of 2 per cent below the overall limit, the Reserve Bank cautions all designated bank branches not to purchase any more equity shares on behalf of FPIs/FIIs/NRIs/PIOs without prior RBI approval. **The Trap:** The caution is triggered at 2% below the limit, not at the limit itself. This acts as an early warning mechanism to prevent inadvertent breaches. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Once on the Caution List, no further purchases can be made without prior RBI approval, providing a buffer to prevent the aggregate limit from being breached.
